description: pymetasploit3 is a Python wrapper for the Metasploit Framework's msgrpc interface.

documentation.example: from pymetasploit3.msfrpc import MsfRpcClient; c = MsfRpcClient('pass'); print(c.modules.exploits)
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/pymetasploit3 && python3 pymetasploit3.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/pymetasploit3 && python3 pymetasploit3.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/pymetasploit3 && python3 pymetasploit3.py --help`. Python client for Metasploit's msgrpc.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the exploit category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python client for Metasploit's msgrpc.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Python client for Metasploit's msgrpc.

metadata.example: from pymetasploit3.msfrpc import MsfRpcClient; c = MsfRpcClient('pass'); print(c.modules.exploits)

name: pymetasploit3

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.
